title: How to Make Moroccan Preserved Lemons with Sea Salt
description: Moroccan preserved lemons are easy to make with just two ingredients: lemons and sea salt. This salt-cured fermentation method produces highly citrusy, floral lemons with umami flavors that are perfect for adding bright flavor to any dish.

ingredients.item: g fresh lemons (about 4 to 5 small lemons)

steps: Wash all of the lemons and weigh out the salt in a bowl.
Cut the ends off the lemons, then starting at one end, cut each lemon into quarters without cutting all the way through.
Over a large bowl, fill the crevices of the lemons with copious amounts of salt until all the salt is used up.
Pack the whole lemons into a clean, large jar, wedging them in as tightly as possible while lemon juice is released in the process.
Once the jar is full, make sure all the lemons are submerged in lemon juice, adding freshly squeezed juice if needed.
Place a lid on the jar and allow the lemons to cure at room temperature for 4 weeks.
Once opened, store in the refrigerator indefinitely, rinsing off excess salt and brine before using, and discarding the pulp while using the rinsed rind as you would use lemon zest.
